projects.title: Driver Behavior Modelling using Model Predictive Control
projects.description: Final Year Project on driver behavior modeling using Model Predictive Control (MPC) and imitation learning. The system learns driving behavior parameters from real traffic trajectories and reproduces expert like vehicle motion in simulation. We evaluated the approach on real-world traffic datasets including the highD Dataset and pNEUMA Vision to model vehicle behavior in traffic scenarios.
projects.caption: Learned MPC controller reproducing expert-like driving behavior, with parameters imitated from real highway trajectories in the highD dataset.

projects.title: Modelling Agressiveness in Driver Behavior using Black Box Simplex Architecture
projects.description: This project models driver aggressiveness using Black-Box Simplex architecture to develop a reactive, behavior-tunable, and safety-aware driver model for autonomous vehicle testing. The ego vehicle is governed by two MPC-based controllers: an advanced controller designed to achieve a target speed, and a baseline safety controller that ensures that the vehicle comes to a complete stop within a specified horizon, enabling effective multi-objective control.
projects.caption: Black-Box Simplex in action: an advanced MPC controller pursues a target speed while a baseline safety controller guarantees the ego vehicle can stop within the horizon.

projects.title: Safe Navigation of Multi-robot Sytstem using Runtime Assurance
projects.description: The Multi-Robot BSA System is a multi-agent robotic coordination framework where multiple autonomous robots operate in a shared environment through continuous motion planning, real-time trajectory optimization, and collision avoidance. Each robot dynamically computes feasible paths under environmental constraints while adapting to the motion of other agents.
projects.caption: Multiple autonomous robots navigating a shared space, continuously replanning collision-free trajectories as they react to one another's motion.

projects.title: Behavior-Based Traffic Simulation using Model Predictive Control
projects.description: Autonomous Traffic Simulation System is a multi-agent traffic simulation framework where each vehicle independently plans its trajectory in real time using Model Predictive Control (MPC), solving a constrained nonlinear optimization problem at every timestep. Vehicles exhibit realistic driving behaviors including lane-following and overtaking, while actively avoiding collisions with neighboring agents.
projects.caption: Behavior-based traffic simulation where each vehicle solves its own MPC problem every timestep, producing emergent lane-following and overtaking without central control.

projects.title: Driver Behavior Analysis using Urban Traffic Data
projects.description: This project focuses on analyzing driver behavior using the [pNEUMA](https://open-traffic.epfl.ch/) urban traffic dataset and drone-based vehicle trajectory data. The study examines real-world driving patterns through metrics such as speed, acceleration, braking behavior, and interactions between surrounding vehicles. By combining trajectory analysis with computer vision-based vehicle tracking, the project aims to contribute to intelligent traffic monitoring, urban mobility research, and data-driven transportation analysis.
projects.caption: Driver behavior analysis from the pNEUMA urban traffic dataset — the same vehicle seen by multiple drones is identified across their overlapping fields of view and registered into a single, continuous trajectory.
